title: Rename final CTA to “Subscription plans” and add Subscription Plans screen

requirements.summary: Rename the landing page final CTA secondary button to “Subscription plans” and navigate to a new Subscription Plans screen via client-side navigation without breaking the existing experience flow.
acceptanceCriteria: On the landing page near the end (Final CTA section), the secondary CTA button text reads exactly “Subscription plans”.
Clicking “Subscription plans” navigates the user to a Subscription Plans screen without a full page reload.
Navigation does not break the existing “Start MindVault” / experience flow.

file_operations.description: Update the secondary CTA button label text to exactly “Subscription plans” and make it clickable by wiring it to a new callback prop (e.g., onSubscriptionPlans) provided by the landing page. Keep the existing Start MindVault button behavior unchanged.

Introduce client-side screen state for showing the new Subscription Plans view (in addition to the existing experience view), add a handler that opens Subscription Plans and pass it down to FinalCTASection. Use browser history state (pushState + popstate handling) so navigation works without a full reload and back navigation returns appropriately, without interfering with the existing Start MindVault/experience back behavior.

requirements.summary: Create a Subscription Plans page/screen showing the three pricing options and a prominent “first session is entirely free” note, with a clear way to return to the landing page.
acceptanceCriteria: The Subscription Plans page shows three plans with prices exactly: “₹200”, “₹800”, and “₹1600”, and includes the corresponding session/time descriptions.
The page includes user-facing text that communicates: “The first session is entirely free.”
The Subscription Plans page provides a clear way to return to the landing page (e.g., a “Go back” control).

file_operations.description: Create a new Subscription Plans screen component that displays: Single session ₹200; ₹800 for 4 sessions in a month; ₹1600 for 12 sessions in 3 months; plus prominent text: “The first session is entirely free.” Use existing layout/primitives (e.g., Section, GlassCard, Callout) for consistent styling, and include a clear “Go back” control (use the existing GoBackButton component).

Wire the new SubscriptionPlansPage into the app by rendering it when the subscription-plans screen state is active, and pass an onGoBack handler that returns the user to the landing content without a full reload (and plays nicely with existing experience view transitions).
